name: Reino's Kalamoiika
recipeIngredient: 4 medium Potatoes, cut into 1- or 1 1/2-inch pieces
2 medium Carrots, cut lengthwise and into half circles about 1/4 inch thick
3 small Onions, cubed coarsely (not chopped fine)
4 Crushed chili tepins (a hot dried pepper)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a 5- or 6-quart pot, bring 1 quart water to a simmer with salt and pepper.
2. Add salmon, leaving skin on. Simmer until the skin comes off easily; set aside on a plate and remove skin and bones.
3. Cut or break salmon into 1- and 2-inch pieces.
4. In the liquid stock, add bay leaves and whole allspices. Add potatoes, carrots, onions, red cabbage, and celery leaves.
5. Cook over low heat until carrots are almost tender and potatoes don't disintegrate.
6. Dice bacon and cook in a fry pan over low heat. Pour off most of the fat.
7. Add butter (or margarine) and diced celery, remaining onion, and green pepper to the fry pan. Saute until onions are yellow and peppers are pale green.
8. Return salmon pieces to the pot. Simmer everything on low heat to preserve flavor.
9. Add clams with broth, shrimp, 1 quart water, pimento, mushrooms, rosemary, cumin, thyme, oregano, and chilies.
10. Stir in milk a cup at a time until fully incorporated.
11. Add butter and let the soup simmer for at least one hour.
12. Serve with tossed salad and garlic bread. Can be frozen.
